jobs.title: Primavera Unifier Solution Architect
jobs.description:
About Us:
Consertus is a global capital program management and advisory firm that combines deep human expertise with advanced digital tools to deliver smarter, faster, and more sustainable outcomes. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, and supported by a team of more than 1,000 professionals worldwide, we help clients plan, execute, and optimize large-scale capital programs.
Our integrated services span digital, advisory, and delivery, empowering organizations to navigate complexity, manage risk, and achieve strategic goals in sectors including aviation, transportation, transit, water, healthcare, energy, education, government, commercial, life sciences, and technology.
At Consertus, we celebrate individual strengths, foster strong relationships, and promote flexibility in how and where we work. Join us to grow your career, develop your skills, and make a lasting impact as we transform how the world is built.
Discover how Consertus is driving transformative change for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve at 
www.consertus.com
  
About this Role: 
The Unifier Solution Architect designs and leads the delivery of Oracle Primavera Unifier solutions that replace manual, paper, and spreadsheet-based processes with a governed, scalable system of record. This role partners with business and project stakeholders to define requirements, translate them into Unifier configurations, and ensure solutions are usable, maintainable, and aligned to program controls and governance.
Key Responsibilities:
Lead the design of Unifier and other PMIS solutions 
Work directly with clients to gather requirements, make recommendations, take detailed notes, and convey those to other members of the project team 
Create strategy documents, such as integration strategy, PMIS strategy, training strategy, etc
Create detailed design documentation, and artifacts
Work with developers and development leads to manage Unifier/PMIS development
Be able to create proof of concepts to demonstrate

About this Role: 
Consertus is seeking a 
Senior Cost Manager/Senior Program Controls Manager
 
(Healthcare Capital Programs)
 based out of 
Seattle, WA.
 In this role, you will wear two hats, own end-to-end cost management while also providing senior-level program controls support to integrate cost, schedule, and risk into clear, decision-ready reporting. This individual contributor role is accountable for budget governance, forecast (EAC) accuracy, and executive-ready insights across multiple projects, partnering closely with project management, healthcare clients, and technical stakeholders to drive financial transparency, risk management, and cost performance.
The ideal candidate brings strong construction cost management experience, knowledge of healthcare environments, and the versatility to move between detailed analysis and program-level controls leadership. You are comfortable switching from building forecasts, validati

jobs.title: Claims and Change Specialist
jobs.location: New York, New York, United States
jobs.description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Claims and Change Specialist
 to support large, heavy-civil infrastructure programs, including highways, roads, bridges, transit (both rail and bus), international airports, and water/wastewater projects delivered through both traditional and alternative methods. Your primary focus will be supporting claims analysis, contract administration, and change management activities associated with complex infrastructure construction projects, including contract research, change control, commercial management support, and coordination with project controls and construction management teams.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in New York, NY.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Support the review of contractor notices, change requests, contract modifications, and related documentation through contract research and claims analysis.

Assist in evaluating entitlement considerations by reviewing contract requirements, technical documentation, project records, and applicable procedures.

Draft correspondence related to contract changes, requests for clarification, and change management activities for management review.

Prepare written summaries, analyses, and supporting documentation outlining contractual considerations, findings, and key project issues.

Support administration of the change control process in accordance with contract requirements, project procedures, and established change management practices.

Review and track change requests to help ensure compliance with contractual requirements, documentation standards, and established timelines.

Compile, maintain, and organize supporting documentation associated with change requests, including cost, schedule, technical, and project controls information.

Maintain accurate records of change orders, approvals, correspondence, negotiations, and supporting documentation within project tracking systems and change logs.

Coordinate

jobs.title: Document Control Lead
jobs.description:
About the Position
 

We’re seeking a 
Document Control Lead 
t
o support a large-scale aviation redevelopment program delivered within a complex public-sector infrastructure environment. This role focuses on maintaining accurate, timely, and compliant project documentation while also supporting the development, implementation, and standardization of program-level document control procedures, protocols, governance materials, etc. 
 

This is a long-term, full-time, hybrid positi
on (4 days per week onsite subject to change based on project requirements) location in Queens, NY.
 

Responsibilities 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:
 

Support implementation and maintenance of program-wide document control protocols and standards.
 

Maintain accurate document naming conventions, metadata tagging, version control, and coding structures in alignment with project and program requirements.
 

Coordinate intake, processing, tracking, distribution, and archival of controlled documents including RFIs, submittals, drawings, specifications, meeting minutes, correspondence, and other project records.
 

Track deliverables and support coordination of document reviews between internal teams, consultants, contractors, and external stakeholders.
 

Prepare and maintain document control reports, logs, transmittals, and tracking dashboards.
 

Perform quality checks on document submissions to ensure compliance with established standards and procedures.
 

Support document retrieval activities for audits, executive reporting, project closeout, and stakeholder requests.
 

Provide user assistance and troubleshooting support within enterprise document management systems such as SharePoint and other EDMS platforms.
 

Support development and maintenance of document control governance materials including procedures, protocols, management plans, and Work Instruction Documents.
 

Assist with standardization and continuous improvement of document control workflows across

jobs.title: Document Control Manager
jobs.description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Document Control Manager
 to support large, complex infrastructure programs, including highways, roads, bridges, transit (rail and bus), international airports, and water/wastewater projects delivered through both traditional and alternative delivery methods. Your primary focus will be leading document control operations, information governance, and enterprise document management initiatives that support program delivery, regulatory compliance, and organizational performance across major capital programs. This role will oversee document control personnel, establish and maintain program-wide standards, and support implementation and optimization of Electronic Document Management Systems (EDMS). The position requires close collaboration with project teams, consultants, contractors, quality management personnel, information technology staff, and executive leadership.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in Sacramento, CA.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Lead, manage, and continuously improve document control operations supporting large, complex infrastructure programs.

Supervise, mentor, and develop document control staff while establishing workload priorities and performance expectations.

Develop, implement, and maintain document control policies, procedures, standards, templates, and governance practices.

Oversee implementation, administration, optimization, and adoption of Electronic Document Management Systems (EDMS) and related document management technologies.

Coordinate document control activities among project teams, consultants, contractors, and stakeholders to ensure consistency and compliance.

Establish and monitor key performance indicators, dashboards, reporting tools, and program metrics to support executive decision-making.

Support business process improvement initiatives, workflow automation efforts, and operational efficiency enhancements.

Collabor

jobs.title: Project Scheduler
jobs.description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Project Scheduler
 to support a portfolio of major transit capital improvement projects. Your primary focus will be developing, maintaining, and analyzing project schedules for the owner's Project Controls team, supporting projects from planning and design through construction and closeout while helping ensure successful project delivery.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in San Jose, CA.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Develop, maintain, and update master schedules, detailed project schedules, schedule forecasts, recovery schedules, and schedule reports for a portfolio of transportation capital projects.

Manage and regularly update schedules for approximately 80 to 100 active projects throughout planning, design, procurement, and construction.

Analyze project schedules to identify critical path activities, schedule risks, constraints, and opportunities for schedule improvement.

Review contractor baseline schedules, monthly schedule updates, recovery schedules, and schedule narratives for compliance with contract requirements.

Perform Time Impact Analysis (TIA) to evaluate schedule impacts associated with contract changes, delays, and change orders.

Coordinate closely with project managers, project controls staff, consultants, contractors, and stakeholders to maintain accurate project schedules and resolve schedule-related issues.

Integrate individual project schedules into larger program or master schedules to support program-level reporting and decision-making.

Support project controls activities involving schedule performance, cost controls, funding, earned value concepts, and project reporting.

Prepare schedule analyses, progress reports, executive dashboards, and other management reports that communicate project status, schedule performance, and forecasted outcomes.

Provide scheduling requirements and schedule specifications for inclusion in procuremen

Project Overview:
 
The project is a 1.3 GW simple-cycle power generation facility located in Millersport, Ohio, being constructed adjacent to a hyperscale data center campus. The facility includes multiple power generation technologies consisting of Solar Titan 130 gas turbines, Siemens SGT800 gas turbines, reciprocating engines, battery energy storage systems (BESS), substations, and associated balance of plant infrastructure. The scope includes major civil, mechanical, electrical, structural, underground utility, control systems, and commissioning works required to support phased energization and turnover of the facility. Construction will be executed through a combination of direct hire and subcontracted labor supported by modularization, pre-assembly, offsite warehousing, and advanced work packaging

Project Overview:
 
Louisiana LNG is located approximately 11 miles southwest of Lake Charles, Louisiana. The project includes a mid-scale, mixed-refrigerant Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) production and export facility on the west side of the Calcasieu River. There are five identical process plants. Each process plant consists of one gas pre-treatment unit, one condensate stabilization unit, and four Chart heavy hydrocarbon (HHC) removal and liquefaction groups. The facility will include associated utilities, refrigerant storage, three 235,000m3 full-containment LNG storage tanks, and three marine berths.

Project Overview:
 
Port Arthur LNG, LLC (PALNG) is developing a Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) production and export facility on a site located on the western shoreline of the Port Arthur Canal in Jefferson County, Texas. The project base scope will include two (2) identical LNG trains, two (2) LNG Storage Tanks, one (1) LNG Carrier Berth, and associated utilities/facilities. The Project is to be constructed on a parcel of land located along State Highway 87 south of Port Arthur, Texas, between the inner coastal waterway bridge and Keith Lake. The Infrastructure Global   Business Unit (GBU) is executing site civil and marine scopes of work associated with our scopes of work on the Energy GBU LNG projects.   As the #1 LNG EPC contractor by installed capacity, Bechtel has the capability to successfully deliver a broad range of LN

jobs.description:
Requisition ID: 296444
 
 
Relocation Authorized: National - Single

Work Location: Brownsville, TX
 
Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:
 
Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown economies, improved the resiliency of the world's infrastructure, increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the world a safer, cleaner place.

Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is partnering with NextDecade to support their vision to provide the world access to lower carbon intensive energy with their Rio Grande LNG (RGLNG) project in Brownsville, Texas. RGLNG is a natural gas liquefaction and export terminal situated on a 984-acre site on the banks of the Brownsville ship channel. The first phase of the RGLNG project consists of three liquefaction trains, two storage tanks and one marine berth. At full capacity, the five-train facility will have a production capacity of 27 million tonnes per annum (MTPA). Brownsville, Texas is a city known for its diverse history, tradition, and rich culture. Located in the southernmost region in the beautiful state of Texas, lays Brownsville a rich, tropical oasis bordering the Rio Grande River and near the shores of the Gulf of Mexico.

We serve the Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security & Environmental; Energy; Mining & Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span from initial planning and investment, through start-up and operations. 
Core to Bechtel is our 
Vision, Values and Commitments
. They are what we believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver. Learn more about our 
extraordinary teams building inspiring projects
 in our 
Impact Report

Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is partnering with NextDecade to support their vision to provide the world access to lower carbon intensive energy with their Rio Grande LNG (RGLNG) project in Brownsville, Texas.
RGLNG is a natural gas liquefaction and export terminal situated on a 984-acre site on the banks of the Brownsville ship channel. The first phase of the RGLNG project consists of three liquefaction trains, two storage tanks and one marine berth. At full capacity, the five-train facility will have a production capacity of 27 million tonnes per annum (MTPA).
Brownsville, Texas is a city known for its diverse history, tradition, and rich culture. Located in the southernmost region in the beautiful state of Texas, lays Brownsville a rich, tropical oasis bordering the Rio Grande River and near the shores of the Gulf of Mexico. B

Project Overview:
 
The rapid growth of AI and digitalization is fueling unprecedented demand for data centers that require large, reliable sources of energy. In response to this market trend, the Renewables & Clean Power (R&CP) team have signed an early works contract to build one of the largest gas fired power generation facilities in the U.S.
Our team is helping the customer progress early-stage development, that would lead to the start of full EPC delivery next year. We are seeking an experienced candidate to be an Area Piping Field Engineer on Project Kilby in Pecos, Texas. The candidate will mobilize on a long-term (camp) assignment to the project in Pecos, TX. This position is contingent upon project award

Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is the licensing, engineering, procurement, and construction partner on TerraPower’s Natrium team. Selected by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for its Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program, Natrium is participating in a DOE initiative to re-establish U.S. leadership in nuclear energy that provides matching funding to industry to design and build an operational advanced reactor. TerraPower, a leading advanced generation technology company founded by former Microsoft CEO Bill Gates, will be deploying its 345 MWe sodium fast reactor (SFR), called Natrium. The Natrium plant features an advanced, cost-competitive reactor design that is safer, simpler, easier, and less costly to construct, less expensive to operate, and able to provide energy that is competitive with fossil fuels. Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is working for Northrop Grumman Corp. to deliver the Engineering, Manufacturing, and Development (EMD) portion of the Sentinel Program.  A system of inter-continental ballistic missiles and launch infrastructure. Sentinel is a vital U.S. Air Force program designed to modernize the nation’s aging force of inter-continental ballistic missiles, which make up the ground element of the nuclear deterrent. As part of Northrop Grumman’s Sentinel team, Bechtel is providing launch infrastructure design, construction, and integration. A major contractor for the United States and allied governments, Bechtel has handled such efforts as chemical weapons demilitarization projects, missile-defense, infrastructure, base operations, procurement and project management, and restoration and recovery spanning half a century. Project Overview:
 
Bechtel Manufacturing and Technology (M&T) is an exciting and growing business that serves the increasing demand of EPC services for customers in the Data Centers, Semiconductor, Pharmaceutical and Electric Vehicle (batteries, charging infrastructure, and production), markets.
M&T strives to exceed our customer objectives through utilizing our agility, innovative technologies, and world class talent. M&T will solve the industries toughest challenges, building both our company and the world for the future.
Headquartered in the U.S. with employees in various states, M&T also has colleagues in the United Kingdom, India, and other locations while we actively pursue exciting opportunities in other parts of the world.
